steps.title: Identify the central claim
steps.content: The passage mentions Carter only as background for the field. Its main point is that Feldman and Stark made a unique contribution by reconstructing early fiscal records and using them to explain Philip II's defaults.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C captures that main idea: Feldman and Stark advanced research on early modern state finance by assembling evidence that gave them insight into Philip II's debt defaults.

Choice A overstates the contrast with Carter. Choice B adds an unsupported explanation involving the logistics of ruling Spain and Northern Catalonia. Choice D overstates the Carter connection; the passage says their work relates to the field that includes Carter, not that it builds on Carter's findings.

The answer is C.
